Name: Joystick
Description: A module that uses potentiometers and a stick to measure values
Why: So you can do stuff to memorize for the minigame

Name: 12mm Button
Description: A tactile switch that either connects a cirucuit or disconnects depending on input
Why: So you can click stuff to memorize for the minigame

Name: 12mm Button Caps (Red Green Yellow Blue)
Description: The caps to indentify which button is which (Will be 3D printer most likely)
Why: To know which button is which.

Name: Potentiometer
Description: A module that uses potentiometers and a stick to measure values through rotation
Why: Another cool function that gives varation and intresting mechanics

Name: TFT LCD 2.4 inch ILI9341 Touch
Description: Display used to show what inputs to press next and as well as score and timer
Why: To see what inputs to do next and other data

Name: ESP-32
Description: The Microcontroller that will control all functions of the minigame as well as logic.
Why: To control the whole minigame and logic

Name: Half Breadboard
Description: A module used to have extra ports and have more leeway in circuit connections
Why: For extra ports to connect GND and PWR and other wires too.

Name: Dupont Female-Male Wires
Description: Used to connect modules to microcontroller and circuits.
Why: Bruh, so the whole thing can get some electricity.

Name: 3D Printer Enclousure.
Description: The host for all modules necessary in the build
Why: To put all of the modules into one area

Name: M7 Jam Nut
Description: 7mm in diameter Jam nut. (Optional because potentiometer has this.)

Name: M4 Screw
Why: To Screw in the Joystick Module and display.
